title: phaeneilema koenigii
intro: Phaeneilema koenigii, also known as Indian gooseberry or Malabar nut, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa, particularly in India and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Phaeneilema koenigii include reducing oxidative stress, improving immune function, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, and its extract is also used in various pharmaceutical and cosmetic products.
